scenarios.description: NO_LLM_SELECTED_SIMULATION decision produces a deterministic context plan with no model involved.
MODEL_SELECTED_SIMULATION referencing an economical simulated entry produces a valid plan.
MODEL_SELECTED_SIMULATION referencing a standard-tier simulated entry with multiple source types produces a valid multi-section plan.
A CONFIDENTIAL source is included when the assembly policy explicitly allows confidential content.
A CONFIDENTIAL source is blocked when the assembly policy does not explicitly allow confidential content.
A RESTRICTED source always blocks assembly regardless of policy.
Source token estimates fit comfortably within the reserved budget, producing an included section with no overflow.
A required source whose estimated tokens exceed the reserved budget hard-blocks assembly under the BLOCK overflow strategy.
Optional sources whose combined estimated tokens exceed the reserved budget are trimmed to fit under the TRIM_OPTIONAL_REFERENCES overflow strategy.
Two sources sharing the same fingerprint are deduplicated, keeping only the higher-priority one.
Two required sources claiming the same content slot with different fingerprints produce an unresolvable conflict.
A source referencing a different tenant than the agent contract blocks the whole assembly.
A source referencing a different organization than the agent contract blocks the whole assembly.
A non-shareable source bound to a different session than the request session blocks the whole assembly.
A memory retrieval reference bound to a different memory contract than the memory contract reference blocks the whole assembly.
A non-acceptable model selection decision status (e.g. BUDGET_BLOCKED) blocks assembly before any planning occurs.
A policy requiring a TASK_REFERENCE section blocks assembly when no task reference source is present.
Sections are ordered deterministically (required first, then priority descending, then section type rank, then section id) regardless of source insertion order.
A structurally valid request intended to be registered twice against the registry with identical payload, asserting REPLAY_ACCEPTED on the second call.
An expected task profile reference version supplied by the caller conflicts with the actual reference version, blocking assembly.
